items.title: Design Read
items.summary: Summarize the surface, audience, outcome, brand posture, foundation, and contextual dials before choosing an aesthetic.
items.intent: Turn an underspecified UI prompt into an explicit, testable context model without forcing the same visual direction onto every product.
checks: Identify the primary user outcome and usage context.
Record variance, motion, density, and information complexity independently.
Name the existing or chosen design foundation.
warnings: Do not expose a long design questionnaire when repository evidence can resolve the decisions.
exceptions: A narrowly scoped visual fix may need only a one-line context read.

items.title: Macrostructure First
items.summary: Choose the page or screen structure and reading order before selecting component recipes or detailed styling.
items.intent: Prevent component-first assembly from producing repetitive card grids and weak hierarchy that do not match the user workflow.
checks: Select a macrostructure that matches the primary outcome.
Verify semantic and visual reading order before styling details.
Define how the structure recomposes at narrow widths.
warnings: Do not choose a structure solely because it appears in a reference gallery.
exceptions: A component-only task inherits the surrounding macrostructure.

items.title: Structural Fingerprint
items.summary: Repeat a controlled set of composition traits so the interface feels authored without repeating identical section templates.
items.intent: Create recognizable visual coherence through heading placement, dividers, action voice, media treatment, rhythm, and reveal behavior.
checks: Name three to six repeated structural traits.
Use controlled variation instead of identical sections.
Confirm the fingerprint does not break content priority or semantics.
warnings: A fingerprint is not a license to apply decoration to every region.
exceptions: Strict platform-native tools may rely primarily on platform structure.

items.title: Visual Ambition
items.summary: Choose a fitting art direction and memorable visual hook so the interface is not merely safe, but visually stronger than a generic baseline.
items.intent: Push public and creative surfaces beyond flat correctness by requiring a product-specific mood, hero object, type treatment, and section rhythm.
checks: Name the chosen visual ambition and primary visual hook before detailed styling.
Use at least one product-specific artifact, subject image, typographic moment, or interactive object near the entry.
Confirm the page is more memorable at thumbnail scale than a generic baseline without adding false claims.
warnings: A truthful page can still fail if it has no strong visual point of view.
exceptions: Strict enterprise tools may prioritize operational clarity over visual drama.

items.title: Visual Craft Guard
items.summary: Refine ambitious visuals so typography, motion, imagery, spacing, and mobile layout feel deliberate instead of generated.
items.intent: Prevent strong concepts from shipping with clipped display type, repetitive giant headings, empty premium space, fake precision, or decorative effects substituting for craft.
checks: Verify display type fits its container and uses intentional line breaks at desktop and mobile sizes.
Use one purposeful motion idea or state-feedback system instead of universal animation effects.
Choose media, generated imagery, or visual artifacts that reveal a concrete subject rather than generic atmosphere.
Vary section rhythm so the page is not repeated oversized headings plus grids.
warnings: A concept can be original and still look generated if execution craft is weak.
exceptions: Strictly functional internal tools may need restrained craft rather than expressive art direction.

items.title: Hero Math
items.summary: Constrain display type, subcopy, action position, and mobile proof so expressive heroes remain readable and useful.
items.intent: Prevent ambitious public pages from shipping with clipped title text, accidental line breaks, hidden calls to action, or mobile first screens that delay the actual proof.
checks: Name the intended desktop title line count and mobile title behavior.
Keep subcopy compact enough to scan and within a readable measure.
Keep the first action and primary proof or hook visible early on mobile.
Use responsive type steps and balanced wrapping instead of brittle hard breaks.
warnings: A poster-style hero can be tall, but it still needs a deliberate crop, action path, and mobile proof.
exceptions: Experimental art pages may prioritize poster impact when the user explicitly asks for that direction.

items.title: Image-First Asset Loop
items.summary: When a page needs a memorable visual subject, create or select one focused asset and design around its composition.
items.intent: Move beyond generic atmosphere by making imagery, crop, light, palette, and subject direction shape the layout before detailed styling.
checks: Define the asset role before generating or curating it.
Use concrete subjects such as products, artifacts, places, materials, or output examples instead of generic ambience.
Extract layout decisions from the image crop, light, palette, texture, and negative space.
Do not present generated people, venues, sponsor walls, awards, or screenshots as real evidence.
warnings: Dropping a generated image into a finished generic template rarely improves the design.
exceptions: Strict UI components, dashboards, and text-first documentation may not need imagery.

items.title: Pre-Delivery Design Critique
items.summary: Score concept, hierarchy, execution, specificity, restraint, and variety before shipping public or de-slop work.
items.intent: Force a final design pass that catches generated-looking outputs even when they are honest, accessible, and technically correct.
checks: Confirm the page has one recognizable idea beyond a clean landing page.
Confirm the first scan reveals subject, proof, and action.
Confirm type, spacing, media, controls, and mobile execution are deliberate.
Revise if adjacent sections repeat the same layout move or the structure could fit any product.
warnings: Do not treat this as a public scoring report unless the user asked for one; use it to improve the work.
exceptions: A narrow component fix may not need page-level critique.

items.title: Stable State Geometry
items.summary: Reserve dimensions for controls, rows, media, and async regions so labels and state changes do not shift the layout.
items.intent: Keep interactions predictable and prevent loading, validation, or long content from moving surrounding controls or obscuring information.
checks: Reserve media space with explicit dimensions or aspect ratios.
Test loading, error, success, and longest-label states.
Keep toolbars, rows, counters, and tiles within stable responsive constraints.
warnings: Fixed dimensions must still allow text reflow and zoom.
exceptions: Content-driven editorial blocks may grow naturally when growth does not disrupt interaction.

items.title: Semantic State System
items.summary: Define color, text, icon, and behavior for default, focus, disabled, loading, error, success, and empty states.
items.intent: Make every reachable state legible and recoverable without relying on color alone or inventing inconsistent local treatments.
checks: Provide visible focus and keyboard behavior for every control.
Pair semantic colors with text, icon, or another non-color cue.
Give failure states a clear recovery path.
warnings: Do not disable actions without making the reason discoverable.
exceptions: Unreachable states do not need artificial examples in production UI.

items.title: Mobile Recomposition
items.summary: Change grouping, order, disclosure, and control layout for narrow screens instead of proportionally shrinking desktop UI.
items.intent: Preserve task priority, reading order, accessible targets, and inspectable content under narrow width and limited height.
checks: Define the narrow-screen content and action priority explicitly.
Keep DOM, focus, and visual order coherent.
Test long labels, keyboards, and short landscape height where relevant.
warnings: Do not hide essential context merely to remove horizontal scrolling.
exceptions: Inherently two-dimensional data may use a labeled horizontal scroll region.

items.title: Proportionate UI Review
items.summary: Tie every UI/UX quality claim to design reasoning, static inspection, existing project checks, or rendered review when practical.
items.intent: Prevent unsupported claims without turning ordinary interface work into an automated browser-testing workflow.
checks: Record which UI/UX dimensions, states, and layouts were actually reviewed.
Separate design critique, static inspection, existing project checks, rendered observations, and automated results.
Use Playwright or other e2e tooling only when the user asks, the project already owns that workflow, or the task is site validation.
warnings: A static source review cannot prove every rendered layout or keyboard behavior; label that limitation instead of forcing automation.
exceptions: During early exploration, label quality conclusions as provisional.

items.title: Real Workflow First
items.summary: Implement the primary task or experience as the first screen instead of surrounding an incomplete feature with marketing content.
items.intent: Focus implementation effort on the user outcome and make operational tools usable immediately.
checks: The first screen exposes the core user action or actual subject.
The primary path works through completion or a truthful bounded state.
Promotional framing does not displace the requested tool or experience.
warnings: Do not omit necessary onboarding when the workflow is genuinely unfamiliar or high risk.
exceptions: A user explicitly requesting a campaign or marketing page may need a hero-led first screen.
